name: Maboroshi Wine
seoDescription: Maboroshi Wine — estate-grown, organically and biodynamically farmed Pinot Noir and Chardonnay in the hills of Sebastopol, Sonoma County. Tasting by appointment.

hero.heading: Burgundy roots, Sebastopol soil
hero.subheading: Tom and Rebecca Kisaichi farm a small estate vineyard in southwestern Sebastopol organically and biodynamically, crafting Pinot Noir and Chardonnay by hand.

about.heading: Our Story
body: Maboroshi means "whimsical dream" in Japanese, and that is exactly what it was for founders Tom and Rebecca Kisaichi. In 1991 they packed up and left for Burgundy with their young daughter. In the Côte d'Or, Tom knocked on doors until he landed work under Charles Rousseau at Domaine Armand Rousseau in Gevrey-Chambertin, while Rebecca studied viticulture.

Back in California, Tom worked his way up through the cellars, from Stonestreet to enologist at Michel-Schlumberger in Healdsburg, while Rebecca worked alongside top winemakers and viticulturists across Napa and Sonoma. In 1999 they planted their own estate vineyard in the Goldridge soils of southwestern Sebastopol and launched Maboroshi.

Today the estate is farmed organically and biodynamically and hand-tended to bring out the true character of the site — two people pursuing their passion for growing grapes and making great wine, remaining true to themselves and to the expression of the land.

services.title: Estate Pinot Noir
services.description: Pinot Noir grown from Dijon selections in the Goldridge soils of the Sebastopol hills, farmed organically and biodynamically.

services.title: Chardonnay
services.description: Small-production Chardonnay made in the same hands-on, terroir-driven style as the estate Pinot Noir.

services.title: Tasting by Appointment
services.description: Intimate, personalized tastings hosted at the estate, often guided by the winemaker himself. Please call or email to arrange a visit.

hours: By appointment only — please call or email

items.quote: We visited a winery built in Sonoma by a Japanese winemaker. I tasted Maboroshi's wine while listening to Mr. Kisaichi's detailed explanation of every step.
items.author: Takuya H., Google reviewer
